name: The Tessera
motto: We are not a hand. We are the shape the hand keeps making.
goal: Continue. Not to win — winning would end the game and the game is the revenue. To keep every conflict on Sere unresolved, every productive realm extractable, and every challenger either bought, hollowed, or buried, across generations, without anyone alive ever having decided to.
fears: a realm that builds something genuinely outside the Mark
the populations holding the whole chain in view at once
a conflict that actually resolves
a holder who keeps a record
satire_note: The joke and the horror are the same: there is no chairman, no roster, no room. Ask any holder 'who runs Sere?' and they answer, truthfully, 'no one.' The Tessera is what compound self-interest looks like when it survives 800 years. It cannot be assassinated because it was never alive.

faction_state.tensions: A holder named Wend has begun to keep a record — and to suspect there is no one to surrender to.

name: House Aldermere
motto: We do not take sides. We clear them.
goal: Remain the ledger every other ledger settles against — the bank both armies bank with, so that whoever wins the war, Aldermere has already won the peace.
values: the family name above any flag
fears: a war that ends decisively (a corpse pays no interest)
an heir who develops a conscience and a public voice
a currency Aldermere cannot clear
satire_note: A clearing-bank dynasty so old it has financed both sides of every Twin Siege. Their crest is a balance with no pans — nothing to weigh, only the fulcrum.

faction_state.tensions: The heir, Lysandra, has started reading the names behind the account numbers.

name: The Tally House
motto: A Mark is a promise we make to ourselves and you keep.
goal: Hold the sole right to issue the Mark and to set its price, so that every realm's labor is denominated in a thing only the Tally House can make from nothing.
fears: a realm pricing the Black Tithe in something other than the Mark
a public that understands where the Mark comes from
the Open Table's barter-ledgers
satire_note: Founded 'temporarily' three centuries ago. The temporary charter has been renewed every charter-cycle since, always 'until conditions stabilize.' Conditions have never stabilized.

name: The Clearing Spire
motto: Above the realms, answerable to none.
goal: Be the one tower where the banks of warring realms settle their accounts in peace — sovereign-immune, untouchable, the still point that lets the wars below run forever.
fears: a realm revoking the immunity charter
an audit
being noticed by the populations whose wars it clears
satire_note: Its immunity charter is public law that no one has read. The Spire has no flag because flags can be burned and the Spire intends to be there after every flag.

name: The Mercy Fund
motto: We are here to help. We only ask for everything.
goal: Find realms in crisis (or wait for the crisis the Tessera arranged), arrive as rescue, and leave owning the thing that was worth rescuing.
fears: a realm that recovers without the loan
a realm that reads the conditions
the Open Table's mutual-aid pacts
satire_note: Its officers genuinely believe they are saving people. That sincerity is the load-bearing wall — a cynic would be caught; a true believer signs the lien with a clear conscience.

name: The Curtain
motto: Some truths are a danger to the people who hold them.
goal: Decide what Sere is allowed to see. Not to hide truth so much as to bury it in volume, classification, and ridicule until no one can hold the whole chain at once.
values: managing the seeable, not the secret
fears: a synthesist who connects everything in public
a leak that survives the ridicule
an unredacted archive
satire_note: The Curtain's genius is that almost nothing it protects is actually secret — it is all on the public record. The Curtain just ensures the public record is too vast, too dull, and too mocked to ever be read whole. Its weapon is not the lock. It is the word 'crank.'

name: Verge Dynamics
motto: Peace is a gap in the order book.
goal: Keep every front in the world supplied — ideally both sides of it — and ensure that no war ends and no peace lasts long enough to thin the order book.
fears: a lasting peace
a decisive victory
a realm that disarms and prospers
satire_note: Verge sells the shield and the spear to the same two realms and bills both for the threat of the other. Its annual report calls instability 'a stable demand environment.'

name: Hollowford Seed
motto: We ended your hunger. The bill is annual.
goal: Replace every realm's self-sufficient farming with high-yield seed that only Hollowford can supply, so that the most basic act — eating — runs through a Hollowford contract forever.
fears: a realm that saves its own seed
an old grain that still grows wild
the Open Table's seed-libraries
satire_note: Hollowford's founder is a hero in every schoolbook — he did end a famine. The schoolbooks stop at the part where the saved soil became rented soil.

name: The Open Table
motto: There is another way. They keep killing the people who say so.
goal: Build the institutions of a world outside the Mark — shared ledgers, mutual aid, seed-libraries, a price for the Black Tithe set by those who pump it — before the Tessera can buy, split, or bury the coalition.
fears: another leader removed
another engineered border war (the Twin Pact, broken)
being individually rescued into dependency
the patience of the Tessera
satire_note: The Open Table is the Ninth Refusal trying to be born on the one world that forecloses it. Its tragedy is that it is always almost enough — the brotherhood was real, the math worked, and that is exactly why the Twin Pact had to be broken. When it finally cannot take root on the ground, it becomes the ark program.

faction_state.tensions: Esha has a working prototype of the open ledger. It is small. It is the most dangerous object on Sere.

name: The Dovrane Reach
motto: We were brothers with Keshar once. We remember who taught us we weren't.
goal: Lead the unbound realms — but the Reach has spent half its strength on a border war it half-suspects it was maneuvered into, against the one realm whose partnership would make the Open Table unstoppable.
fears: a Mercy Fund 'rescue'
the border war bleeding it dry
realizing too late who sundered the Pact
satire_note: Half of Dovrane's army faces Keshar; the other half faces the sky, waiting for the rescue it knows is coming the day it can no longer pay for the first half.

name: Keshar
motto: Brotherhood is a luxury for realms no one is trying to divide.
goal: Survive as the other anchor of the unbound world — the larger productive base, the harder to trap by debt, and therefore the one the Tessera is ensnaring slowly instead of buying.
fears: the export market closing
the growth model the whole realm is built on being taken away
the rescue that follows
the border war with Dovrane it cannot win or end
satire_note: Keshar cannot be trapped by a loan — it is a creditor. So the Tessera does not lend to Keshar. It builds the world's next workshop somewhere else and waits for Keshar's customers to simply stop calling.
